title: EXP-S substrate KEYSTONE — kind-tagged coexisting-index substrate (row_kind + step-16 migration + per-kind index dispatch + determinism check)

commits.subject: feat(0.8.14): row_kind column + step-16 migration (EXP-S R-SUB-1/R-SUB-3)
commits.summary: Schema crate: forward-only migration step 16 (SCHEMA_VERSION 15->16) adds canonical_nodes.row_kind TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'leaf' with the MIGRATION-ACCRETION-EXEMPTION marker. New tests/step16_migration.rs; bumped step-count/SCHEMA_VERSION tripwires in migrations.rs, step14_migration.rs, engine compatibility.rs.

commits.subject: feat(0.8.14): row_kind index-target dispatch + determinism check (EXP-S R-SUB-1/R-SUB-2/D2/D5)
commits.summary: Engine crate: pub RowKind {Leaf,Coverage,Graph}; row_kind->index-target LOOKUP seam (index_targets_for_row_kind + project_canonical_node_row); commit_batch Node arm refactored to write row_kind='leaf' via the seam (behavior-identical for leaf); internal write/query seams (write_canonical_row_with_kind_for_test, canonical_rows_with_row_kind_for_test); tests/exp_s_substrate.rs (R-SUB-1, D2 index-target divergence, R-SUB-2 determinism).

acceptance.R-SUB-1: GREEN — row_kinds (leaf/coverage/graph) coexist; migration lands the column; fixture writes 3 distinct row_kinds and the engine selects by row_kind (schema s16_row_kind_column_present..., engine r_sub_1_row_kinds_coexist_and_are_queryable_by_row_kind).
acceptance.R-SUB-2: GREEN — incremental multi-index write is deterministic; two fresh DBs, drained to quiescence, produce byte-identical serialized index state (FTS content rows, vec0 rows incl. quantized embedding BLOB, _fathomdb_vector_rows, row_kind tags, projection-terminal cursors) via engine r_sub_2_incremental_multi_index_write_is_deterministic.
acceptance.R-SUB-3: GREEN — migration is forward-only + guarded: schema s16_forward_only_from_v15_defaults_existing_rows_to_leaf (open at v15 with a pre-existing row, apply ONLY step 16, reach v16, legacy row defaults to 'leaf', exactly [16] ran) and s16_passes_accretion_guard_only_with_marker.
acceptance.D5-TC-1: DISCHARGED (hygiene) — the per-row_kind index-target set is a wrappable LOOKUP seam (index_targets_for_row_kind) with an ADR-0.8.14 §D5 / ledger TC-1 code comment; FTS/filter writes stay same-transaction and vector writes stay async-via-worker; terminal-cursor readiness stays per-kind-extensible. NO OPP-12 surface implemented.

tdd.note: Schema tests authored against a not-yet-migrated schema (SCHEMA_VERSION=15) would RED on the s16_* / bumped-count assertions before step 16 existed; committed as an atomic migration+tests change. Engine tests reference RowKind + the internal seams so they are committed test+impl together (memory: 'clear test+impl commit' acceptable). All new tests verified GREEN post-impl.

targeted_tests: cargo test -p fathomdb-schema => ok (all migration + step16 tests pass)

cargo test -p fathomdb-engine --test compatibility --test batch_write_per_row_cursor --test projection_runtime --test cursor_read_after_write --test pr_g0_identity => all ok (regression, node write path)
cargo test -p fathomdb-engine --features operator --test dump_schema --test rebuild_projections --test rebuild_vec0 --test excise_source --test source_id_writes => all ok
verification.agent_long_not_run: eu7 recall gate NOT run (Slice 20 concern per D6; correct scope for this slice).

d6_vec0_rewrite.detail: Migration step 16 is a pure additive ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMN on canonical_nodes; it does NOT touch vector_default / vec0 rows and does NOT reshape ensure_vector_partition. No embedder, pooling, or sign-bit quant change. Per ADR-0.8.14 §D6 the eu7 fidelity gate at Slice 20 is therefore a DOCUMENTED NO-OP (no re-embed, no re-quantize). ANN fidelity is mathematically invariant.
d6_vec0_rewrite.eu7_action_for_slice_20: Run recall_gate::recall_ci_clears_floor(ci_hi, 0.90) as a documented no-op regression check only; no breach expected.

scope_boundaries_respected.no_public_sdk_surface_added: true — row_kind is engine-internal: PreparedWrite::Node unchanged (132 construction sites + Py/TS bindings untouched); Leaf via column default for all normal writes; coverage/graph via internal for_test seams. No Python/TS binding changed.
scope_boundaries_respected.doc_type_kind_vocabulary_untouched: true — resolve_source_type, KIND_TO_SOURCE_TYPE_CASE_SQL, migration-9 preflight CHECK all unchanged; row_kind is a SEPARATE column/axis (D1).

next_step_for_orchestrator: Slice 5 COMPLETE at head 400369e44a85313595bd04aeb8174b679c09a60c. Ready for codex review (schema migration + engine seam) then cherry-pick onto the integration branch. Downstream: Slice 10 (F5 fielded BM25F, step-17 migration) rides these row_kind/field columns; Slice 20 runs the eu7 no-op per D6 (record vec0_rows_rewritten=false above). NOTE for Slice 40 / X1: RowKind + row_kind currently have NO Py/TS binding — cross-binding parity is deferred to Slice 40 (MAIN-tree build) as the ADR §Consequences states.
